eulaBody:
GENERAL TERMS OF SALE AND USE (T&Cs)

Article 1 - Company identification
Company name: SMHRJU
Registered office: 78 Avenue des Champs Elysees, 75008 Paris, France
Registration number: 100957091
Purpose: Platform connecting pilgrims with guides for the organisation of religious services (Omra, guided tours, Omra Badal).

Article 2 - Contractual documents
Use of the application and subscription to a service implies unconditional acceptance of these T&Cs as well as the following documents, permanently available for consultation in the application:
- The Pilgrim Charter.
- The Religious Guide Regulations.
- The Guide Code of Conduct and Operational Rules.

Article 3 - Service description
The application enables pilgrims to book guiding services in Makkah and Medina, including religious accompaniment, historical visits and the performance of Omra Badal.

Article 4 - Commitments of the parties

Pilgrim commitments:
The pilgrim undertakes to provide accurate information, to respect schedules, safety instructions and the ethical principles of Islam.

Guide commitments:
The guide undertakes a mission of public interest based on the Quran and Sunnah. They are obliged to wear their official vest for identification and to observe strict punctuality.

Article 5 - Specific service provisions

Omra Badal:
The guide is obliged to produce and transmit two videos (entry into ihram and end of mission) to attest to completion.

Pickup:
If the hotel is less than 500m from Masjid Al-Haram, the guide goes there directly. Beyond this, a meeting point is agreed upon mutually.

Article 6 - Prices and payment terms
Prices are indicated in the application at the time of booking. Payment is made exclusively online via a secure external platform. No bank details are stored by the application. Payment validation constitutes acceptance of the mission by the guide and of these conditions by the pilgrim.

Article 7 - Liability
The pilgrim remains responsible for their personal belongings, health and compliance with local laws.
The guide is responsible for the faithful execution of their mission (Amanah) before the pilgrims and before Allah.
The company SMHRJU acts as an intermediary and cannot be held liable in cases of force majeure or actions attributable to the user.

Article 8 - Intellectual property
All application content (logos, interfaces, texts) is the exclusive property of SMHRJU. Any reproduction or lending of user/guide accounts is strictly prohibited.

Article 9 - Applicable law and disputes
These T&Cs are governed by French law. Any dispute relating to their interpretation or execution falls under the jurisdiction of the courts of Paris.

Contact: support@nefsy.app
privacyBody:
PRIVACY POLICY (GDPR STANDARD)

Protecting your data is a priority. We process your information in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Data collection and use

Registration data:
We collect your identity information and documents necessary for organising the mission.

Health:
The pilgrim is responsible for reporting any important medical information for their own safety during the journey.

Meeting organisation and logistics:
No real-time geolocation data (GPS) is collected by the application. Meeting and pickup points are agreed directly between the pilgrim and the guide via the secure messaging system. The pilgrim provides the necessary logistical information (hotel name, room number for people with reduced mobility) to enable the service.

Media and proof:
For Omra Badal services, multimedia files (videos and photos) are collected and transmitted to attest to the completion of the rites in accordance with the guide's commitments.

Data sharing:
For the smooth running of the mission, the necessary information (name, specific needs, proof media) is shared exclusively between the pilgrim and the relevant guide. No data is sold to commercial third parties.

Internal messaging and confidentiality

Access:
Messaging between the pilgrim and the guide is enabled only after payment validation.

Use:
Exchanges must be limited exclusively to the spiritual and logistical framework of the mission.

Retention:
In accordance with the GDPR data minimisation principle, messages are not permanently retained. They are deleted from our active servers after the mission closes, unless required for dispute resolution.

Ethics:
Guides are strictly prohibited from using this messaging system to divert clients or offer private services outside the platform.

Security and user rights

Protection:
No bank details are stored by the application, as transactions are processed by a secure external payment provider.

Hosting:
Your data is stored on secure servers with a professional hosting provider guaranteeing a high level of protection and confidentiality.

Rights:
You have the right to access, rectify and delete your data.

Account deletion:
In accordance with Apple and Google requirements, an account deletion button is available in the application settings, resulting in the permanent erasure of your personal data (except for legal retention obligations).

Account responsibility
The user is solely responsible for the confidentiality of their credentials. Any substitution, lending or sharing of accounts is a serious breach of the trust commitment (Amanah).

Contact: support@nefsy.app
cancellationBody:
CANCELLATION AND REFUND POLICY

This policy applies to any service booked via the application. Purchasing a service implies unconditional acceptance of these conditions.

Applicable deadlines and fees

Early cancellation (more than 48h before):
Any service may be cancelled free of charge up to 48 hours before its start, entitling you to a full refund.

Late cancellation (less than 48h before):
For any cancellation made less than 48 hours before the service, a 25% deduction of the total amount is applied. These fees correspond to the management, booking and organisation already undertaken.

Request procedures

Procedure:
Cancellation requests must be made in writing via the official communication channel of the application.

No-shows:
In the event of pilgrim absence or unjustified delay, no refund will be granted, as the service is considered to have been initiated.

Force majeure
In the event of an exceptional occurrence (justified serious illness, administrative decision or unforeseeable event), each situation will be examined with fairness and care by our team.

Contact: support@nefsy.app
